June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:pages to redo the thing on]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Kangoku-chō 6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}}

June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|qq=|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Kangoku-chō 6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}}

June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Kangoku-chō 6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Kangoku-chō 6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Kangoku-chō 6, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Manjū]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
