The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Ministry of the Imperial Household]], but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the [[:en:Siege of Nara|Burning of Nara]] led by [[:en:Taira no Shigehira|Taira no Shigehira]]. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and [[:en:Tajimamori|Tajimamori]], this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]]: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]]: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11565493]] Wikidata name: Kangō Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Kangoku-chō 6, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Kangoku-chō 6, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:qq]]

June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, Kangoku-chō 6, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara|1=Siege of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira|1=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kangō-jinja]][[ja:漢國神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kangō Shrine | location = {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, Kangoku-chō 6, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683611|N|135.825417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Nara | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) • Former Prefectural Shrine | consecration_year = Suiko 1 (593) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (three bays, cypress bark roof) | alternate_name = Manju Shrine | festival = October 17 | Rituals = April 19: Manju Matsuri
June 5: Chinhanasai
June 15: Setsuyōshu Festival
June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|WD=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara|1=Siege of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|WD=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira|1=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|WD=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|WD=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

June 17: Saikusa Festival{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City Historical Compilation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=1985|page=179|isbn=4642015493}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kangou-jinja, shaden.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Kangō Shrine''' (漢國神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor, disputable site) and a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == The shrine’s origins are traced to Suiko 1 (593), when {{ill|Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja|大神君白堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} established the enshrinement of Sonokami by imperial decree.{{efn|According to the "Chronicle of the Three Deities of Ōmiwa," Ōmiwa no Kimi Shiratsutsumi enshrined deities at two sites: Spring Hill (Harugawa) and Sakaoka.}} Later, in Yōrō 1 (717), {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|qid=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} added two Korean deities to the main shrine. The shrine’s former names include "Sonokamikami Shrine" and "Sonokami Shrine," with "Kangami" becoming "Kangō" due to linguistic shifts. In Jōgan 1 (859), the shrine’s deities were reportedly enshrined in Kyoto’s {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, but discrepancies exist between this claim and the records of Sonokami and Kangami shrines in Kyoto. The shrine suffered destruction in 1181 during the {{ill|Siege of Nara|en|Siege of Nara|qid=Q1116528|lt=Burning of Nara|1=Siege of Nara}} led by {{ill|Taira no Shigehira|en|Taira no Shigehira|qid=Q710537|lt=Taira no Shigehira|1=Taira no Shigehira}}. It was later rebuilt in 1188 under the guidance of the high priest of Kōfuku-ji Temple. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|qid=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:漢國神社]] [[ja:林神社]] {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. During the Keichō era (1596–1615), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted five tan of land in Hōren Village to support the shrine. In the Meiji period, Kangō Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} The shrine venerates Ōmononushi as Sonokami and Ōkuninushi and Sukunahikona as Karakami. According to the shrine’s legend, the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} in Kyoto enshrined these deities, designating them as {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Grounds == * '''Main Hall''': A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with cypress bark roofing, designated a Nara Prefecture tangible cultural property. * '''Rin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Hayashi Jōin|ja|林浄因|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashi Jōin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志ほせ饅頭|1=Shiose Manjū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86726873}} and {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|qid=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}}, this shrine honors the "ancestor of manju" and "ancestor of confectionery." ** On April 19, the shrine holds the "Manju Festival," where sweets manufacturers offer their goods, and visitors receive free manju and tea.[http://narashikanko.or.jp/event/index.php?m=d&id=56 Manju Festival at Kangō Shrine] Nara City Tourism Association * '''Treasury''': Contains a suit of armor donated by Tokugawa Ieyasu, now a Nara City-designated cultural property. * '''Other Shrines''': Includes {{ill|Aoi Shrine|ja|葵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dedicated to Tokugawa Ieyasu; {{ill|Gennkurou Inari Shrine|ja|源九郎稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gennkurou Inari Shrine|1=Genkurō Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563977}}; and various other subsidiary shrines. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nara Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Main Hall (designated in 1951)Nara City Board of Education informational panel. * '''Nara City Tangible Cultural Property''': ** Armor (now housed at the Nara National Museum). == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}: 2 minutes on foot from Exit 7-S. * {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}}: 11 minutes on foot from the East Exit. * By bus: Disembark at "Kintetsu Nara Station" and walk 3 minutes. == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nara City Board of Education informational panel == External Links == {{commonscat|Kangō Shrine}} * [http://www.kangou-jinja.jp/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|漢國神社|version=100231532|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kangō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11565493|co|commons:Category:Kangō-jinja|ja|漢國神社|ja|林神社}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Manjū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
